meaning: adj.不同的,不相似的 prep.不象...,和...不同
examples: It's very unlike him to be so late.
This was a foreign country, so unlike San Jose.
The sound was not unlike that of birds singing.
Unlike TIPS, which are taxable, the municipal bond version offers a real aftertax return.
Unlike their cousins in Australia, they have no interest in nibbling on a human.
